headline: Binance CEX is a fully custodial exchange with no on-chain smart-contract surface; 8 Ethereum EOA hot/cold wallets and 1 BSC hot wallet confirmed via Etherscan/BscScan name-tags; no GitHub repo, no audit PDFs, and no deployed-contracts doc were found.
short_headline: 8 ETH EOA wallets; no contracts discovered

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search returned 8 Binance-labelled EOA wallet addresses on Ethereum (Hot Wallet 20, Binance 7, Binance 14, Binance 16, Binance 18, Hot Wallet, Hot Wallet 2, and classic Binance wallet). BscScan returned 1 BSC hot wallet (0x631fc1ea). Non-EVM chains (BTC, XRP, DOGE, LTC, HBAR, NEAR, TON, XLM, RON, APT, etc.) have no EVM block explorer and were not enumerable via Etherscan-family tools; recorded in unknowns.

Binance has no public 'Deployed Contracts' doc because it is a CEX with no user-facing smart-contract protocol. The closest analogue is the 2022 transparency blog post (binance.com/en/blog/community/our-commitment-to-transparency-2895840147147652626) and the live Proof of Reserves page (binance.com/en/proof-of-reserves), which list custody wallet addresses per chain (BTC, ETH, BSC, BNB, TRX) but are not a deployment registry. Set deployed_contracts_doc to the PoR blog post as the best available analogue.

No audit PDFs linked in protocol.audit_links (field is empty). No third-party smart-contract audit reports were surfaced in searches. Binance does conduct Proof-of-Reserves attestations (monthly, per search results) but these are reserve attestations, not smart-contract audits. Recorded in unknowns.

protocol.github is null and no Binance CEX GitHub repository was found in searches. The exchange does not publish deployment artifacts because it has no on-chain smart-contract protocol. Recorded in unknowns.

EVM chains covered: Ethereum (8 EOAs via Etherscan), BSC/Binance chain (1 EOA via BscScan). Non-EVM chains in protocol.chains (Bitcoin, Ripple, Doge, Tron, Litecoin, Hedera, Near, TON, Stellar, Aptos, Ronin, Polkadot, Sui, Algorand, Fantom, Chiliz, Celo, Sonic, Optimism, Arbitrum, Base, zkSync Era, Scroll, Manta, Op_Bnb, Starknet, Avalanche, Polygon) were not enumerable via EVM block explorers; Tron and other addressed chains are noted in unknowns. Binance published wallet addresses for BTC, ETH, BSC, BNB, TRX networks in the 2022 transparency post.
No factory contracts exist; Binance CEX is a custodial exchange, not a DeFi protocol. No factory-child enumeration applicable.

All discovered addresses are EOA custody wallets (hot or cold) — none are smart contracts, multisigs, timelocks, or governance contracts. Role taxonomy applied: all entries use role 'treasury' or 'other' with actor_class 'eoa'.
All 9 admin_addresses entries trace to Etherscan or BscScan search results fetched in this run. No snippet-only sightings were promoted without a block-explorer citation.

rationale.verdict: Binance CEX is a fully centralized custodial exchange with no on-chain smart-contract protocol surface. Discovery walked Etherscan name-tag search (D1), the official transparency/PoR blog (D2), audit links (D3, none found), GitHub (D4, none found), and multi-chain explorers (D5). Nine EVM-addressed EOA custody wallets were catalogued. The vast majority of chains in protocol.chains use non-EVM architectures not enumerable via the available toolset, and are recorded in unknowns.

evidence.shows: Binance is the world's leading cryptocurrency exchange with 270M+ registered users; users can trade spot, margin, futures, and options markets; exchange holds user crypto in custodial wallets.

DeFiLlama sources Binance wallet addresses from the transparency blog post at binance.com/en/blog/community/our-commitment-to-transparency-2895840147147652626; Binance is described as the largest CEX by daily trading volume.

Binance publicly disclosed hot and cold wallet addresses as of November 2022 across BTC, ETH, BSC, BNB, TRX networks as part of a transparency commitment; this is the canonical wallet address reference used by DeFiLlama.

Binance operates a live Proof of Reserves page confirming user assets are held at least 1:1; corporate holdings are in separate wallets not included in PoR calculations; SAFU emergency fund exists; no smart contracts are deployed for the CEX itself.
Etherscan name-tag: 'Binance: Hot Wallet 20'; EOA with $39B+ balance across 18 chains as of Apr-18-2026.
Etherscan name-tag: 'Binance'; EOA custody wallet for Binance exchange on Ethereum.
Etherscan name-tag: 'Binance 14'; EOA custody wallet present on multiple EVM chains (BSC, Base, Polygon, Blast, Arbitrum, Optimism, Avalanche).
Etherscan name-tag: 'Binance 7'; EOA custody wallet on Ethereum.
Etherscan name-tag: 'Binance 18'; EOA custody wallet on Ethereum and multiple EVM chains.
Etherscan name-tag: 'Binance 16'; EOA custody wallet on Ethereum and multiple EVM chains including zkSync Era, Scroll, BSC, Polygon, Base, Arbitrum.
Etherscan name-tag: 'Binance: Hot Wallet'; EOA confirmed on Ethereum with small balance.
Etherscan name-tag: 'Binance: Hot Wallet 2'; EOA on Ethereum.
BscScan name-tag: 'Binance: Hot Wallet'; EOA with 10.6M transactions on BSC as of Jan-19-2026.
Binance operates a bug bounty program on Bugcrowd.

Binance CEO blog explaining wallet architecture: deposit wallets per user, hot wallets for operational use, cold wallets for large reserves; Binance uses proprietary wallet infrastructure rather than third-party multisig solutions.

unknowns: D1: Non-EVM chains (Bitcoin, Ripple/XRP, Doge, Litecoin, Hedera, Near, TON, Stellar, Aptos, Ronin, Polkadot, Sui, Algorand, Fantom, Sonic, Chiliz, Celo) have no EVM block explorer; wallet addresses on these chains were not enumerable with available tools in this run
D1: Tron chain Binance hot/cold wallet addresses not enumerated in this run (Tronscan not searched)

D1: Optimism, Arbitrum, Base, Scroll, zkSync Era, Manta, Op_Bnb, Starknet, Avalanche, Polygon Binance wallet addresses not individually verified via per-chain explorers in this run (some addresses e.g. 0x28c6c0... appear on multiple EVM chains per Etherscan cross-chain balance display, but per-chain address roles not separately confirmed)
D2: No formal deployed-contracts documentation page exists; binance.com/en/proof-of-reserves and the 2022 transparency blog are the closest analogues but are not a structured deployment registry

D3: No smart-contract audit PDF links recorded; Binance PoR attestations are reserve attestations by Mazars/others, not smart-contract audits — audit firm names and report URLs not confirmed in this run
D4: No GitHub repository found for Binance CEX; protocol.github is null and confirmed absent
D5: chain Bitcoin: zero EVM addresses surfaced; non-EVM chain not enumerable via available tools
D5: chain Ripple: zero EVM addresses surfaced; non-EVM chain not enumerable
D5: chain Doge: zero EVM addresses surfaced; non-EVM chain not enumerable

D8: SAFU fund wallet address not confirmed via a fetched URL in this run (DeFiLlama notes it is excluded from PoR calculations and tracked separately)

protocol_metadata.about: Binance is the world's largest centralized cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume, serving over 270 million registered users across 180+ countries. Users can buy, sell, and trade hundreds of cryptocurrencies via spot, margin, futures, and options markets using a traditional order-book model. Binance operates as a fully custodial exchange, holding user assets in proprietary hot and cold wallets and publishing monthly Proof of Reserves attestations using a Merkle-tree approach. It also maintains a Secure Asset Fund for Users (SAFU), an emergency insurance fund funded by a percentage of trading fees.
